title: Auto-Research-Trading Codebase Knowledge Graph
description: Machine-readable network map of the Auto-Research-Trading repository showing major modules, runtime surfaces, capabilities, and key relationships.

nodes.label: Research Harness
nodes.description: The fixed historical-data and backtest engine centered on prepare.py and backtest.py. It defines the canonical scoring path and core shared dataclasses for the repo.

nodes.label: Strategy Surface
nodes.description: The mutable strategy.py seam where trading logic is changed. It consumes shared market and portfolio dataclasses and emits target-position signals.

nodes.label: Intraday Validation
nodes.description: The 5-minute validation and replay-oriented path centered on backtest_5m.py. It evaluates faster bar data and writes richer artifacts for tactical strategy review.

nodes.label: Benchmark Suite
nodes.description: The fixed set of reference strategies in benchmarks/ executed by run_benchmarks.py. It provides comparison baselines against the mutable strategy.

nodes.label: Paper Engine
nodes.description: The simulated execution and persistence layer formed by paper_engine.py, paper_trade.py, and paper_state.py. It reuses the strategy contract for replay and paper operation.

nodes.label: Jupiter Live Runner
nodes.description: The orchestration entrypoint in run_jupiter_live.py for paper mode and guarded live mode. It bridges strategy target positions into Jupiter-facing execution flows.

nodes.label: Jupiter Execution Adapter
nodes.description: The jupiter_execution.py translation layer that converts repo target deltas into Jupiter CLI-backed order plans. It is the privileged execution backend for local-wallet live mode.

nodes.label: Jupiter Market Data Adapter
nodes.description: The jupiter_live_adapter.py module that polls public Jupiter data and synthesizes bars. It feeds live or paper trading loops with market snapshots.

nodes.label: Workbench Server
nodes.description: The local operator dashboard in fly_entrypoint.py. It exposes a small HTTP control plane and supervises managed subprocesses for paper feed and experiment orchestration.

nodes.label: Workbench CLI
nodes.description: The workbench_ctl.py client that calls the local dashboard API. It is a control surface for status inspection and supervised process actions.

nodes.label: Workbench Auth
nodes.description: The workbench_auth.py helper and auth-related fly_entrypoint integration. It implements signed-cookie login for the local or Fly-hosted workbench surface.

nodes.label: Experiment Manager
nodes.description: The continuous validation and experiment cycling surface in experiment_manager.py and related artifacts. It manages experiment states, iteration output, and candidate evaluation.

nodes.label: External Wallet Bridge
nodes.description: The external_wallet_bridge.py review surface for JSONL order handoff files. It keeps signing outside the repo process while preserving a review trail.

nodes.label: Reporting and Exports
nodes.description: The export and reporting utilities such as export_equity.py, export_milestones.py, and generate_charts.py. They turn backtest and experiment outputs into CSV and chart artifacts.

nodes.label: State and Artifacts
nodes.description: The filesystem state under ~/.cache/autotrader and repo artifacts/ outputs. It stores caches, workbench status, paper state, JSONL handoffs, and generated validation results.

nodes.label: Docs Surface
nodes.description: The docs/ guidance layer containing execution rules, backtest guidance, governance specs, and agent harness instructions. It defines many repo operating contracts.

nodes.label: Agent Skills
nodes.description: The repo-local .agents skill surfaces, including art-auto-research and g-kade. They guide how agents interpret and operate the repo’s workflows.

edges.description: Research Harness depends on Strategy Surface because the backtest runners load strategy.py as the main experimentation seam.
Research Harness compares against Benchmark Suite so the mutable strategy can be evaluated against fixed baselines.
Research Harness shares dataclass and scoring semantics with Intraday Validation while changing time resolution and artifact detail.
Strategy Surface feeds Paper Engine because replay and paper loops call Strategy.on_bar and use its target-position output.
Strategy Surface feeds Jupiter Live Runner because even live-capable orchestration still begins from repo-native strategy signals.
Jupiter Live Runner depends on Jupiter Market Data Adapter for public market snapshots and bar synthesis.
Jupiter Live Runner depends on Jupiter Execution Adapter when translating target deltas into live execution plans.
Jupiter Execution Adapter emits JSONL order requests to External Wallet Bridge in external-wallet mode.
Workbench Server reads persisted paper state and supervises the paper-feed subprocess.
Workbench Server launches the Jupiter Live Runner as the managed paper-feed process.
Workbench Server launches and controls Experiment Manager as a managed subprocess.
Workbench CLI calls the Workbench Server HTTP API for status and control actions.
Workbench Server protects dashboard routes with Workbench Auth while leaving health checks available.
Paper Engine persists state into cache-backed JSON files and reads from them across sessions.
Experiment Manager writes control, status, event, and cycle artifacts into workbench cache and repo artifact paths.
Reporting and Exports read result tables and generated run artifacts to produce CSV and chart outputs.
Docs Surface documents the harness rules, commands, and validation expectations used by the research workflow.
Docs Surface governs how paper, live, and external-wallet Jupiter flows are supposed to be used.
Agent Skills consume and reinforce the repo guidance and execution contracts written in docs.
Agent Skills provide operator-oriented guidance for controlling the dashboard and its supervised processes.
